title: Op Checkmate: 30 Indians held for illegally working as truck drivers in US, deportation likely
content: US Border Patrol's Operation Checkmate has led to the arrest of approximately 30 Indian nationals working illegally as commercial truck drivers in Arizona. These individuals, along with others from different countries, are facing deportation. The operation aimed to enhance public safety by enforcing immigration laws and identifying undocumented drivers operating heavy vehicles.

title: South Korea overtakes India to become world’s sixth largest stock market; here’s why
content: In the cases of both South Korea and Taiwan, the stock market rally is fuelled by a powerful surge in semiconductor giants that are benefiting from the global artificial intelligence boom.

title: Banned, injured, forgotten: Rasikh Salam Dar never stopped believing
content: Rasikh Salam Dar's rise from a two-year ban and career-threatening injury to becoming a key figure in RCB's title-winning campaign is a story of resilience and belief. Guided by family, shaped by hardship, and driven by an unwavering dream, the Kashmiri pacer has earned redemption.

title: Did ChatGPT put children at risk? Why Florida has sued OpenAI and Sam Altman
content: Florida has sued OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman, alleging they knowingly marketed ChatGPT despite risks to children and the public. The lawsuit claims the company prioritized growth over safety, failing to implement safeguards for minors and contributing to harm, including self-harm and criminal planning. OpenAI disputes these allegations, citing its safety measures.

title: United Airlines flight makes U-turn after 4-hours in air over suspicious Bluetooth device name
content: A United Airlines flight from Newark to Spain made an unexpected U-turn after a passenger's Bluetooth speaker, named "BOMB," triggered a security scare. The teenager responsible had reportedly forgotten about the name. Authorities evacuated the plane and inspected it thoroughly, causing significant delays and passenger concern.

title: 'People think I love to hit every ball, but ...': Sooryavanshi's answer to Ashwin
content: Young batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi dominated IPL 2026, earning the Most Valuable Player award with a record 72 sixes and 776 runs. The explosive opener, known for his fearless approach, expressed his ambition to excel in Test cricket, a sentiment echoed by former chief selector MSK Prasad who urged his immediate inclusion in the Indian squad.

title: After CBSE OSM row, NTA’s re-examination portal comes under cyber security spotlight
content: The National Testing Agency’s (NTA) re-examination portal has come under scrutiny after cybersecurity researchers alleged vulnerabilities that could expose administrative data and provide unauthorized access to portal functions. The claims, shared online, remain independently unverified, and NTA has yet to respond to queries seeking clarification. The allegations emerge amid heightened concerns over the security of examination-related digital platforms following the recent controversy surrounding CBSE’s On-Screen Marking (OSM) system.

title: 'Arrest me if you want': Mamata accuses BJP of using police to break TMC
content: Mamata Banerjee challenged the BJP to arrest her, accusing them of attempting to dismantle the Trinamool Congress through intimidation and bribery. She vowed to protest on June 2, even without official permission, and threatened to move the demonstration to Delhi. Banerjee also alleged widespread election rigging and police complicity in suppressing dissent in Bengal.

title: Americans are saying 'no' to lucrative yet stressful job promotions!
content: We are witnessing a profound shift in workplace priorities as American employees place greater emphasis on mental wellness and the balance between work and personal life, overshadowing the pursuit of high salaries. A striking study reveals that nearly 80% associate their jobs with adverse mental health outcomes, resulting in numerous professionals now rejecting lucrative roles that offer no wellness incentives.

title: 'Amazon would collapse if run like New York City': Jeff Bezos takes swipe at Mamdani
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has endorsed New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's new government efficiency initiative, COGE. This comes after their public disagreement on taxing the wealthy. Bezos, who previously criticized city spending, now supports Mamdani's plan to make government work smarter, suggesting savings could benefit lower earners.

title: 'Everybody hates you now': Trump spars with Netanyahu over Israeli strikes on Lebanon
content: President Trump reportedly called Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu "crazy" and accused him of ingratitude during a heated phone call over Israel's escalation in Lebanon. Trump allegedly blocked a plan to strike Beirut, citing concerns over civilian casualties and disproportionate escalation, despite acknowledging Israel's right to self-defense.

title: Google plans to release 32 million Wolbachia-infected mosquitoes across Florida and California
content: Alphabet's Debug initiative is seeking EPA approval to release 32 million specially treated male mosquitoes in California and Florida. This innovative approach uses Wolbachia bacteria to sterilize wild Culex mosquitoes, aiming to significantly reduce the spread of diseases like West Nile virus. Advanced AI and robotics are enabling this large-scale effort, with trials planned over two years.

title: An alternative to the Strait of Hormuz? Why Iran is eyeing Yemen's Bab al-Mandab
content: Escalating Middle East tensions are now focusing on the Bab al-Mandab Strait, a crucial global shipping lane. Iran, via its Yemeni allies, may use this chokepoint to pressure adversaries, potentially disrupting vital oil and goods transit. This raises fears of wider conflict impacting international trade routes and energy markets.

title: ITR filing: Top reasons salaried taxpayers should wait till mid-June to file IT returns
content: Many salaried taxpayers are already gearing up to submit their returns, hoping to finish the process early, receive refunds sooner or avoid the usual rush closer to the deadline. But before rushing to file, there is one crucial question taxpayers should consider.

title: 'I had Rs 84 in my account and would bow to the bank every day': Gaurav Gera on struggles
content: Actor-comedian Gaurav Gera is currently enjoying the love coming his way following the success of Dhurandhar and Dhurandhar: The Revenge. But the journey to this moment was far from easy. In a recent conversation, Gaurav opened up about his struggles, career choices, financial hardships and the lessons that have kept him grounded.

title: Kelce caught in fresh Blake Lively–Taylor Swift drama rumors
content: Taylor Swift’s upcoming wedding to Travis Kelce has sparked renewed attention on some of her high-profile friendships. Reports claim Swift has grown distant from former close friend Karlie Kloss, while her relationship with Blake Lively remains under scrutiny following the actress’s legal dispute with Justin Baldoni. Sources suggest Swift has become more selective about her inner circle, prioritizing trust and long-term loyalty as she prepares for the next chapter of her life with Kelce.

title: Cowboys, Eagles Eye Blockbuster move for $160M Defensive Standout
content: The Dallas Cowboys and Philadelphia Eagles were among the teams linked to former Cleveland Browns star Myles Garrett before the Los Angeles Rams completed a blockbuster trade. Cleveland received rising pass rusher Jared Verse, a 2027 first-round pick, and additional draft assets. Garrett, whose contract is worth roughly $160 million, is expected to receive a new deal in Los Angeles as the Rams strengthen their championship hopes.

title: JEE Advanced 2026 topper Shubham Kumar: 'No social media helped me secure AIR 1'
content: Shubham Kumar, an 18-year-old from Bihar, has secured the top rank in JEE Advanced 2026. His disciplined two-year preparation in Kota, marked by avoiding social media, limiting phone use, and consistent study, led to his success. He plans to pursue Computer Science at IIT Bombay, highlighting willpower and focus as key to achieving his goal.

title: CBSE launches online re-evaluation portal amid OSM tender controversy
content: In a proactive move to enhance transparency, CBSE has unveiled a new online portal where students can check their marks and request re-evaluations of their answer sheets. This initiative is supported by an informative video guide and is a response to recent investigations by the Education Ministry into potential discrepancies in on-screen marking and cybersecurity matters.

title: Norway Chess: R Praggnanandhaa, Divya Deshmukh back to winning ways
content: Round 7 of Norway Chess saw Indian stars bounce back after a difficult previous round. R Praggnanandhaa defeated Alireza Firouzja in a commanding classical game, while Divya Deshmukh beat Koneru Humpy in an Armageddon tie-break to remain second in the women’s standings. Meanwhile, Magnus Carlsen and D Gukesh won their Armageddon games but remained at the bottom of the open-section leaderboard.

title: Top stocks to buy or sell: Stock market recommendations for June 2 - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: Infosys has been recommended as a top buy call by Somil Mehta, Head of Retail Research at Mirae Asset ShareKhan on June 2, 2026. The expert has recommended DLF, and Cholamandalam Investment and Finance Company as sell calls. Target prices, stop loss and ranges have been shared.

title: Afghanistan to host India for T20I series in September
content: An official announcement is awaited, but the matches are slated to be played on Sept 13, 16 and 19. Afghanistan are touring India for a one-off Test and three ODIs starting from June 6.

title: 80kg dummy, 2-hour re-enactment: How CBI tested Giribala’s version in Twisha death case
content: CBI recreated Twisha Sharma's alleged suicide, using a dummy to test the plausibility of ex-judge Giribala Singh and her son's account of bringing her down. Investigators are examining the house layout, timing, and forensic evidence. The probe also includes scrutinizing the son's movements and potential evidence tampering, while prison authorities consider security for the former judge.

title: India-US deal: $140bn trade ties move closer to new framework agreement
content: India and the US have finalized most of the legal text for their bilateral trade agreement, with negotiations now focusing on minor details. The treaty's finalization hinges on clarity regarding the US tariff mechanism post-next month, as India seeks to maintain export advantages.

title: Karnataka: DKS's cabinet to debut with 10-20 ministers; Delhi talks continue
content: Congress leaders held late-night discussions in New Delhi to finalize the new Karnataka cabinet, with 10-20 ministers expected in the first phase. The initial list is nearly ready, with final decisions to be presented to party chiefs Kharge and Rahul Gandhi. Discussions also covered key organizational appointments.

title: 'Bhuvneshwar has surprised me about how outstanding he is': Flower
content: In the last two IPL seasons, Bhuvi has taken a combined 45 wickets, including 28 wickets this season. It is his best IPL performance, going close to his IPL 2016 (23 wickets) and IPL 2017 (26 wickets) tallies. In the last two seasons, no one has been as lethal as Bhuvi with Prasidh Krishna (41 wickets) coming closest. Among the bowlers who've bowled at least 100 overs, his economy (8.55) is only marginally behind Noor Ahmed (8.27). This season he finished just one short of eventual Purple Cap winner Kagiso Rabada.

title: If you chop off a limb, people will follow law: Karnataka HC
content: A Karnataka High Court judge's strong remarks on crime deterrence, suggesting "chopping off a leg or a hand" for compliance, have sparked debate. Refusing bail to a 23-year-old engineering student accused of rape, Justice R Nataraj commented on the perceived leniency in the justice system. The court has issued a notice to the state government regarding the matter.

title: Quote of the day: Michael Jordan
content: Michael Jordan's career was shaped as much by doubt and scrutiny as by championships and accolades. From being cut from his high school team to enduring criticism over his leadership, gambling habits and retirement, he repeatedly faced narratives questioning his ability and character. Rather than respond publicly, Jordan relied on self-belief and performance, turning criticism into motivation and ultimately rendering the rumours irrelevant.

title: England football captain Harry Kane's special post for Virat Kohli and RCB
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru secured their second consecutive IPL title, defeating Gujarat Titans in the 2026 final. England captain Harry Kane congratulated Virat Kohli and RCB on their historic back-to-back championship wins. Skipper Rajat Patidar also joined an elite list of captains to achieve this feat, crediting Kohli for his support.

title: Wangchuk says key points omitted from Ladakh talks draft, warns of hunger strike
content: Fresh disagreements have emerged regarding Ladakh's political future. The Leh Apex Body claims the Centre omitted crucial details from a draft record of recent talks. They are demanding corrections and warning of renewed protests, including hunger strikes. The Ladakh chief secretary stated the summary document was shared in good faith and invited objections.

title: 'Isse achha kya khelega?' Jadeja pushes for Sooryavanshi's India debut after IPL heroics
content: Speaking after Sooryavanshi's remarkable IPL season, Jadeja questioned what more the youngster could possibly do to prove his readiness for higher honours.

title: 'Serious differences with leadership': Annamalai to quit BJP, launch new party - Exclusive
content: Sources indicate former Tamil Nadu BJP president K Annamalai is set to quit the party, with a meeting with top BJP leaders in Delhi expected to precede an announcement. This development follows mounting speculation about his political future, fueled by supporter posters and his own cryptic remarks.

title: With 5 judge appointments, SC's strength increases to 37
content: The Law Ministry has appointed five new judges to the Supreme Court. This brings the total strength of the apex court to 37, including the Chief Justice of India. The new judges will take their oath on Tuesday. This appointment fills most of the vacancies after the sanctioned strength was recently increased. The Supreme Court now has one vacancy remaining.

title: In security talks, Rajnath, Australian defence minister advocate stable Indo-Pacific
content: India and Australia emphasized freedom of navigation and a free, open Indo-Pacific during their Defence Ministers' Dialogue. The two nations are advancing bilateral maritime security cooperation, including joint maritime domain awareness activities and exploring enhanced undersea domain awareness, to bolster regional peace and security.

title: Won’t allow territory to be used against India: Myanmar president
content: India and Myanmar have strengthened ties, with President U Min Aung Hlaing assuring New Delhi that Myanmar's territory won't be used against India's security. Discussions focused on trade, border security, and defence cooperation. India reiterated its commitment to Myanmar's sovereignty and territorial integrity, emphasizing the need for peace and inclusion in the country's ongoing peace process.

title: India reviews duty sop on Scotch in UK trade deal
content: India is reviewing tariff concessions on Scotch and other goods in its trade deal with the UK. This action is contingent on the UK addressing India's concerns regarding steel quotas and duties. UK Trade Secretary Peter Kyle is set to meet with India's Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal to resolve this deadlock.

title: Trump says Iran talks continue 'at rapid pace' as Tehran suspends negotiations after Lebanon strikes
content: US President Donald Trump stated that negotiations with Iran are progressing rapidly despite recent exchanges of fire testing a fragile ceasefire. He also announced a halt to attacks between Hezbollah and Israel following calls with Prime Minister Netanyahu and Hezbollah representatives. Iran, however, indicated it has ceased communication with Washington through mediators due to the escalation in Lebanon.

title: New audio deepens concerns around Josh Jacobs’ arrest investigation
content: Green Bay Packers running back Josh Jacobs is facing increased scrutiny after newly released dispatch audio revealed reports of yelling, screaming, and objects being thrown inside his Wisconsin home before his arrest. Authorities later booked Jacobs on multiple domestic abuse-related charges, including a felony count of strangulation and suffocation. Jacobs has denied all allegations through his attorneys, who say key evidence has yet to be made public as the investigation continues.

title: No urgent SC hearing on PIL for computer-based NEET re-exam
content: The Supreme Court refused an urgent hearing for a PIL seeking a computer-based test for the NEET-UG re-examination. The bench noted past dismissals of similar pleas and posted the matter for July, citing authorities' challenges in conducting the re-exam quickly. The plea was tagged with other petitions seeking NTA reforms.

title: Venezuela acting president may visit India next week
content: Venezuela's acting President, Delcy Rodriguez, is poised for a New Delhi visit this week, signaling a deepening energy partnership. This comes as India actively seeks to diversify its oil sources, with Washington encouraging increased purchases from the South American nation.  Venezuela has recently emerged as a significant crude supplier for India.

title: SC: Rape survivor shouldn’t be repeatedly called to court
content: The Supreme Court has protected rape survivors from repeated court appearances for cross-examination.  Quashing a High Court order, the apex court emphasized that forcing victims to face further questioning years after initial testimony inflicts unjustifiable hardship.  Such repeated appearances, especially in sensitive cases, can cause undue distress to victims of heinous crimes.

title: SC asks Tamil Nadu govt to act against forest encroachments, fines 118 babus
content: The Supreme Court has ordered the Tamil Nadu government to prepare a time-bound action plan within a month to evict thousands of encroachers from the Agasthyamalai biodiversity hotspot. The court also mandated disciplinary and legal action against 118 identified government servants involved in forest land encroachment, warning of administrative accountability for non-compliance.

title: Indian women using internet doubled to 64% in 4 years
content: India is experiencing a significant shift in women's digital access and education. Internet usage among women has nearly doubled, while preschool attendance and years of schooling for women have also seen notable increases. However, the survey highlights persistent urban-rural disparities in female school attendance, indicating ongoing challenges despite national progress.

title: Sri Lankan Tamil girl killed after gang rams SUV into two-wheeler on Chennai’s Koyambedu flyover
content: A six-member gang first picked a fight with her and then assaulted her before driving the SUV into her two-wheeler, say the police

title: Purported audio of Minister attempting ‘sabotage’ of Davanagere bypoll goes viral
content: The Davangere bypoll preparations had turned tricky for the Congress leadership amid rumours that sections of Muslims, upset over denial of ticket to a candidate from the community, might turn against the party

title: CBSE opens verification and re-evaluation portal
content: CBSE said the portal was live during the early hours on Tuesday (June 2, 2026), missing its original deadline of June 1, 2026; several users on X complained that they were unable to login

title: I came to know about my brother’s death only through Google search: Chamba accident victim’s sister
content: A tourist taxi carrying seven visitors from Bengaluru and a local driver is feared to have plunged into a deep gorge along the treacherous Bairagarh-Sach Pass-Killar road in the remote Churah subdivision of Himachal Pradesh’s Chamba district on the night of May 29, leaving all eight occupants feared dead

title: After partial collapse, push to restore Radhakrishnan heritage site in Mysuru
content: Located in Saraswathipuram, the bungalow served as residence of former President Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan when he taught philosophy at the University of Mysore

title: ‘Outsiders’ lobbying to enter Rajya Sabha from Karnataka Congress
content: Y.S. Sharmila, Supriya Shrinate, and Pawan Khera from outside Karnataka are said to be lobbying with the Congress leadership for a Rajya Sabha berth from the State

title: Sharmila slams TDP for criticising Y.S. Rajasekhar Reddy, Y.S. Raja Reddy
content: Andhra Pradesh Congress Committee president asks why the TDP did not act against the two leaders when it was earlier in power, and accuses the ruling coalition of ‘cheap politics’

title: Telangana statehood Day: Committed to supporting State’s growth trajectory, says PM Modi
content: Established on June 2, 2014, it became India’s 29th state after being carved out of the northwestern region of Andhra Pradesh

title: Undertrial prisoners entitled to be treated at private hospitals of their choice: Madras High Court
content: Justices G.R. Swaminathan and V. Lakshminarayanan permit Devanathan Yadav of Mylapore Hindu Permanent Fund Nidhi Limited to undergo treatment at a private hospital for 10 weeks

title: Prisons in India continue to be overcrowded by undertrials | Data
content: More than half of the States/UTs had an occupancy rate of over 100% in 2024 despite a modest increase in capacity

title: The Iran war and the uncertain future of the expats
content: The Gulf will continue to matter for India, but the old certainty of the “Gulf dream” has been shaken

title: West Asia crisis clouds monsoon tourism hopes of Kumarakom
content: Stakeholders say there is no influx of Arab travellers to the backwater destination; industry pins hopes on travellers from north India for its revival

title: Twelve years of dedication, a legacy of achievement
content: The fact that Narendra Modi’s Prime Ministership and his government have been in place for 12 years is a direct indication of the public’s steadfast faith in him

title: Government notifies changes in Immigration and Foreigners Rules, 2025
content: The new provision will replace the previous rule that required the registration "within fourteen days after the expiry of one hundred and eighty days of arrival in India."

title: CJI Surya Kant to administer oath to five new Supreme Court judges
content: The Registry has made special arrangements for the ceremony and notified officers, staff members, and coordinating agencies, including the Supreme Court Security and the CPWD

title: 30 Indians living and working illegally in U.S. as truck drivers arrested, will be deported
content: Operation Checkmate is aimed at enhancing public safety through enforcement of immigration statutes to detect and arrest illegal persons operating commercial motor vehicles

title: Supreme Court seeks Centre’s response on plea over non-compliance with disability panels’ recommendations
content: The plea sought the top court’s intervention to address ‘existing gaps’ in the functioning of these ‘oversight bodies’ and ensure ‘meaningful’ protection of disability rights across India

title: Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam | The star who stormed into politics
content: The political organisation built by actor-turned-politician Vijay, which emerged as the single largest party in the Assembly, declares itself as a secular movement, rooted in the Tamil experience, and committed to social justice and equality

title: Chief Minister Vijay promises inclusive and corruption-free government
content: The TVK government will never compromise on secularism, State rights, sharing of river water, or social justice, he says at a public meeting organised to thank the voters of Tiruchi East, from where he was elected to the Assembly, besides Perambur in Chennai

title: Doctors’ body barred from Parliamentary panel meeting on NEET after BJP objections
content: UDF representatives, listed as witnesses, stopped from deposing before panel reviewing National Testing Agency amid row over neutrality

title: No urgent relief to Cockroach Janata Party over X ban: Delhi HC
content: Justice Purushaindra Kumar Kaurav says such relief can only be granted after hearing the government as the issue has far-reaching consequences

title: CJP founder to return to India on June 6, launch ‘peaceful protest’ seeking Pradhan’s resignation
content: In a video released on Instagram, Abhijeet Dipke called on supporters and students to join a peaceful protest in Delhi seeking the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan

title: Cockroach Janta Party now transitioning into a movement: Abhijeet Dipke
content: Mr. Dipke said he would return to India on June 6 and seek permission for a protest at Jantar Mantar, the CJP’s first major planned public mobilisation

title: Supreme Court declines plea to hold NEET re-test in computer-based mode
content: Top court expresses disinclination to change mode of June 21 re-test and defers consideration of matter until July, after conclusion of court’s partial working days

title: Trinamool MLAs facing threats, not being allowed to attend party meetings: Mamata
content: The statement comes a day after only one fourth of MLAs showed up at a party meeting which led to the event being called off

title: Cuddalore government school toppers now get to learn scuba diving
content: The camp, which began last week at Temple Adventures, has attracted 27 boys who emerged with flying colours in their Plus Two exams

title: Court allows CBI to arrest former Reliance ADAG executive Jhunjhunwala in bank fraud case
content: After hearing arguments from both sides, the Mumbai court allowed the CBI's application to formally arrest the accused as per provisions of law.

title: The silken voice of Hindi cinema falls silent
content: From vibrant chartbusters to melancholic ghazals, the versatile singer ruled hearts without chasing the spotlight

title: ED attaches assets worth ₹1,595 crore in PACL case
content: The properties, belonging to Gian Sagar Educational & Charitable Trust, are located in Punjab’s Ramnagar. In the same case, the ED has so far attached assets valued at about ₹28,626 crore, in India and abroad.

title: Every eligible student in every village should be able to pursue higher education, that is our government’s goal: T.N. Higher Education Minister
content: Highlighting the role of facilitation centres set up across the State to provide assistance to students during the admission process, the Minister said the centres are manned by experienced teachers

title: Man dies after alleged custodial torture in Odisha; three police personnel suspended
content: The BJD alleged that nine custodial deaths were reported in the past year matching the total number recorded over the preceding four years

title: Breakaway faction of banned Jamaat rallies behind J&K L-G Manoj Sinha’s anti-drug campaign in volatile Kulgam
content: “When we choke off drug smuggling, we weaken those who seek to destabilise our nation, spill the blood of innocent people, and divert our youth from the path of progress,” says the Lieutenant Governor

title: Meghalaya and Nagaland CMs call for release of 20 hostages
content: Conrad Sangma writes to apex social bodies of Nagas and Kukis of Manipur for immediate release of all hostages; Neiphiu Rio seeks Home Minister’s intervention for release of 6 Liangmai Nagas

title: Overloading, violation of SOPs have made Kashmir’s Gondola cable car vulnerable to technical snags
content: Set up in 1998, Gondola has two phases of rides: one from Gulmarg to Kongdoori (10 minutes ride) and another from Kongdoori to Apharwat (12 minutes ride), with the highest point located at an altitude of 4,390 metres (14,403 feet).

title: Book Seller At Dawn, Illegal Gun-Maker At Dusk: Racket Busted In Karnataka
content: The police have seized nine country-made pistols and a large quantity of firearm components and ammunition-related materials

title: "Everybody Hates You Now": Trump's Brutal Dressing-Down Of Netanyahu
content: US-Iran War Latest News: "You're F***ing Crazy, Would Be In Prison If It Weren't For Me": Trump Lashes Out At Netanyahu Over Lebanon

title: Won't Allow Our Territory To Be Used Against India: Myanmar President
content: India and Myanmar on Monday pledged to deepen bilateral cooperation across security, trade and infrastructure as Prime Minister Narendra Modi held talks with Myanmar President U Min Aung Hlaing in New Delhi

title: Weather Today LIVE: Delhi Records Coolest Start To June In 3 Years
content: Weather Updates Today, Heatwave, IMD Alert LIVE Updates: Delhi experiences lowest June temperatures in three years and moderate air quality index of 105.

title: Trump's "Eternity" Hope For Israel And Iran-Backed Hezbollah
content: Trump said on his Truth Social network that Hezbollah had "agreed to stop shooting at Israel, and its soldiers. Likewise, Israel agreed to stop shooting at them. Let's see how long that lasts -- Hopefully it will be for ETERNITY!"

title: Pentagon Bars Journalists From Its Press Office. Here's Why
content: On X, acting Pentagon press secretary Joel Valdez confirmed the move, saying there was "nothing controversial" about it and that it came because speechwriters, who use classified material, were now occupying the space.

title: 6.1 Magnitude Earthquake Hits Italy
content: A 6.1 magnitude earthquake struck off the coast of southern Italy in the early hours of Tuesday, the German Research Centre for Geosciences said. The quake was registered at a depth

title: Deal With Iran On Truce, Hormuz Expected "Over The Next Week", Says Trump
content: He told ABC News he has thus far withheld final approval on a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) aimed at unblocking the vital shipping lane, explaining, "I still have to get a few more points."

title: 17-Year-Old Boy Stabbed To Death In Delhi, Probe On: Cops
content: A 17-year-old was allegedly stabbed to death in northeast Delhi's New Usmanpur, the police said on late Monday.

title: UAPA Accused Gets Bail After Nearly 5 Years In Custody
content: While granting bail, the court observed that the accused, Madhesh Shankar alias Abdullah, has been in judicial custody for more than four years and nine months.

title: Hezbollah Accepts US Proposal For Mutual Halt To Attacks With Israel: Lebanon
content: Lebanon's embassy in Washington said in a statement on Monday that Hezbollah had accepted a US proposal for a mutual cessation of hostilities that would be extended to encompass all Lebanese territory.

title: "Endangering Our Kids": Florida Sues OpenAI Over ChatGPT Safety Risks
content: Florida's attorney general on Monday sued OpenAI and its CEO Sam Altman, accusing the company's ChatGPT chatbot of endangering young users by making them addicted and encouraging harmful behaviors.

title: Amid Public Fury, Trinamool Leaders Return 'Cut Money' In Bengal Village
content: The distribution took place at a field, where the villagers were called for a special gathering. In some cases where Trinamool leaders are on the run, their families came to pay up.

title: Weather Highlights: Drastic Drop In Temperatures Keeps Delhi Cool
content: Weather Updates Today, Heatwave, IMD Alert LIVE Updates: Weather experts attributed the cooler conditions to rain and thunderstorm activity associated with an ongoing western disturbance over Rajasthan and central Pakistan.

title: 'Maharashtra Model' In Bengal? Buzz Over New Trinamool Congress Without Mamata Banerjee
content: A vast majority of Trinamool Congress MLAs were absent from a scheduled meeting at Mamata Banerjee's house

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Iran Says Suspending US Talks, Trump Claims The Opposite
content: US-Iran war live updates: The remark by Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf came as reports suggested US President Donald Trump has sent back a revised, tougher peace proposal

title: IPL Betting Racket Busted In Guwahati During Final, 8 Arrested
content: The main accused, Alok Jain, has been running an IPL and cricket betting racket for a considerable period of time.

title: Iran Threatens "New Fronts" In War As Peace Talk Cliffhanger Continues
content: Iran's news agency Tasnim reported Tehran had suspended dialogue with mediators in protest at Israel's expanding offensive in Lebanon.

title: 80-Kg Dummy, Missing Belt: Cops Check Forensic Details In Twisha Sharma Case
content: The agency is also examining allegations that Twisha Sharma may have been assaulted before her death and whether any evidence was altered afterward.

title: Who was Dave Fiji? Indian-origin pilot killed in helicopter crash hours after marrying
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers.
